ddls:
CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "time_generated_utc" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access attempt was generated (UTC) */,
  "document_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the document */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access attempt occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the application */,
  "browser_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the browser */,
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the operating system */,
  "security_rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the security rule */,
  "document_protection_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the document protection policy */,
  "access_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access status */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the access attempt (e.g., Success, Fail) */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the security rule applied */,
  "rule_url" TEXT /* URL of the security rule (if applicable) */,
  "json_extra" TEXT /* Additional details in JSON format */,
  "sender" TEXT /* Sender of the access log (e.g., NCProtect) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_access_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_status_id") REFERENCES access_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_os_id FOREIGN KEY ("os_id") REFERENCES operating_systems ("os_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_browser_id FOREIGN KEY ("browser_id") REFERENCES browsers ("browser_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_document_protection_id FOREIGN KEY ("document_protection_id") REFERENCES document_protection ("protection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_document_id FOREIGN KEY ("document_id") REFERENCES documents ("document_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_security_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("security_rule_id") REFERENCES security_rules ("security_rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documents" (
  "document_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the document */,
  "document_url" TEXT /* URL of the document */,
  "sha512_hash" TEXT /* SHA512 hash of the document (if available) */,
  "document_protection_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the document protection policy */,
  "document_name" TEXT /* Name of the document */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of the document (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  "document_location" TEXT /* Location of the document (e.g., SharePoint, network drive) */,
  "document_size" INTEGER /* Size of the document in bytes */,
  "document_uploaded_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the document was uploaded */,
  "document_modified_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the document was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("document_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_display_name" TEXT /* Display name of the user */,
  "user_login_name" TEXT /* Login name of the user */,
  "user_email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "application_type" TEXT /* Type of the application (e.g., web, desktop) */,
  "application_version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "browsers" (
  "browser_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each browser */,
  "browser_name" TEXT /* Name of the browser */,
  "browser_version" TEXT /* Version of the browser */,
  "browser_type" TEXT /* Type of the browser (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("browser_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operating_systems" (
  "os_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operating system */,
  "os_name" TEXT /* Name of the operating system */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "os_type" TEXT /* Type of the operating system (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_rules" (
  "security_rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security rule */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the security rule */,
  "rule_url" TEXT /* URL of the security rule (if applicable) */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the security rule */,
  "rule_type" TEXT /* Type of the security rule (e.g., access control, encryption) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("security_rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "document_protection" (
  "protection_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each document protection policy */,
  "protection_name" TEXT /* Name of the document protection policy */,
  "protection_description" TEXT /* Description of the document protection policy */,
  "protection_type" TEXT /* Type of the document protection policy (e.g., encryption, access control) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the access status */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Description of the access status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trails" (
  "audit_trail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit trail */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access log */,
  "disposition" TEXT /* Disposition of the access attempt (e.g., allowed, blocked) */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the access attempt (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the audit trail was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_trail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES access_logs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access log */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of the event (e.g., security breach, access denied) */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES access_logs ("log_id")
)
